title: Santaloides
intro: Santaloides, also known as East Indian sandalwood or Santalum album, is a slow-growing, evergreen tree native to the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in reducing stress, improving skin health, and enhancing cognitive function. The bioactive constituents of Santaloides include sesquiterpenes, such as santalol and santalene, which are responsible for its distinct fragrance and medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Santaloides include essential oils, capsules, and powders, which are often used in aromatherapy, skincare routines, and herbal supplements.
taxonomy: Santaloides belongs to the genus of flowering plants in the family Santalaceae. This plant is native to Africa, and its classification is often debated among botanists. Santaloides is a part of the larger group of plants known as sandalwoods, which are known for their distinctive sandalwood scent.
